frothy session — run the structured editor and host-compile session

Session is the structured serial path for editors and host-assisted compilation. It accepts .source/.end-source blocks, can compile Frothy source on the host when the device advertises host-optional mode, replay a recorded transcript, or emit NDJSON records. Human terminal use should normally start with connect.

Examples:
  frothy session --records --port /dev/cu.usbserial-0001
      emit the structured session records consumed by editor clients

  printf '.source main.fr\ninclude "helper.fr"\nmain:\n.end-source\n' | frothy session --port /dev/cu.usbserial-0001
      send unsaved source text as one editor-owned block

Flags:
  -baud int
    	serial baud rate (default 115200)
  -compiler string
    	C overlay compiler helper
  -dry-run
    	print device lines without opening serial
  -file string
    	load source lines from a file, applying boot definitions last
  -host-compile
    	use host compiler when the device advertises host-optional mode
  -port string
    	serial port, for example /dev/cu.usbmodem101
  -records
    	emit NDJSON session records on stdout
  -replay string
    	replay accepted source from an NDJSON record transcript
  -settle duration
    	delay after opening serial (default 2s)
  -timeout duration
    	serial prompt timeout (default 3s)
  -transcript string
    	write NDJSON session records to a file; requires --records
